title: DeepSeek shorthand resolution and API-key handling
description: Map deepseek, deepseek-v4-pro, and deepseek-v4-flash shorthands to openai-compat at https://api.deepseek.com, add a DEEPSEEK_API_KEY environment fallback to the openai-compat key chain, and give a DeepSeek-specific missing-key error in validateApiKey when the resolved endpoint is api.deepseek.com.

successCriteria.description: resolveProviderModel("deepseek-v4-pro") returns provider "openai-compat", modelId "deepseek-v4-pro", and endpoint "https://api.deepseek.com", asserted by a unit test.

resolveProviderModel("deepseek") and resolveProviderModel("deepseek-v4-flash") each return openai-compat with the api.deepseek.com endpoint and the corresponding modelId (deepseek-v4-pro and deepseek-v4-flash respectively), asserted by unit tests.

resolveProviderModel("ollama/llama3") still returns openai-compat at http://localhost:11434/v1 and resolveProviderModel("sonnet") still returns anthropic — proving no regression to existing shorthands.

When the resolved endpoint is api.deepseek.com and neither providerConfig.apiKey nor process.env.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is set, validateApiKey throws an error whose message contains the string DEEPSEEK_API_KEY; a unit test asserts this.

When DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is set in the environment, an OpenAICompatAdapter constructed for the deepseek endpoint passes that key to the mocked openai client constructor (no key-missing error), asserted by a unit test with the openai client mocked.
npm run build exits 0 and npm run lint exits 0 after the changes.

nonGoals: Do not map deepseek-reasoner or deepseek-chat shorthands (they deprecate 2026-07-24).
Do not hardcode any API key value anywhere in source.
Do not make DEEPSEEK_API_KEY apply to non-deepseek openai-compat endpoints (Ollama must keep its no-key behavior).
Do not add the openai package as a hard dependency.
stopConditions: All six success criteria pass: deepseek shorthands resolve to the api.deepseek.com endpoint, the DEEPSEEK_API_KEY fallback and DeepSeek-specific missing-key error work, no existing-shorthand regression, and build plus lint exit 0.
definitionOfDone: A user can write model "deepseek-v4-pro" (or "deepseek", "deepseek-v4-flash") in bober.config.json and the resolver points it at https://api.deepseek.com through the openai-compat adapter. agent-bober reads the key from providerConfig.apiKey or DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, and when both are absent for a deepseek endpoint it fails with a message that names DEEPSEEK_API_KEY rather than silently skipping like Ollama. Existing Ollama and anthropic resolution is unchanged.
assumptions: Q1: only the v4 deepseek line is mapped — evidence: PRD Open Questions leans v4-only, task instructions confirm 'map only the deepseek v4 line'.
Q4: DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is special-cased keyed on the api.deepseek.com endpoint — evidence: PRD FR-3 specifies exactly this; per-vendor configurable key-env is a Non-Goal.
Tests mock the openai client so no real network call or key is needed — evidence: PRD Technical Considerations 'Tests must mock the openai client (deepseek)'.
generatorNotes: Add to SHORTHAND_MAP in src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts entries for deepseek, deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-v4-flash. Because SHORTHAND_MAP entries currently only carry {provider, modelId}, you must extend resolveProviderModel to attach endpoint https://api.deepseek.com for these (mirror how the ollama/ branch returns an endpoint). In src/providers/openai-compat.ts the apiKey already flows to OpenAIAdapter; add the DEEPSEEK_API_KEY fallback either in OpenAICompatAdapter (when endpoint includes api.deepseek.com) or in factory.ts createClient's openai-compat branch (line ~179-195) and in openai.ts getClient key chain (line ~289-293). In factory.ts validateApiKey, the openai-compat case (line 87-89) currently no-ops; add: if endpoint/resolved target is api.deepseek.com and no apiKey and no process.env.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, throw a DeepSeek-specific error. Mock the openai client in tests (vi.mock).
evaluatorNotes: Run npm test and confirm new tests in model-resolver.test.ts cover all three deepseek shorthands plus a no-regression assertion for ollama/ and sonnet. Confirm factory.test.ts (or openai-compat.test.ts) covers: key-missing throws with DEEPSEEK_API_KEY in the message, and key-present passes the key to the mocked client. Run npm run build and npm run lint and assert exit 0. Verify no real network call occurs (openai client is mocked).
